name: Cable Car Barn & Powerhouse
note: Archive documents linked to this place; source labels honour the SFPL-vs-Democracy's-Library distinction.

documents.title: Strategic plan 2000 : short range transit plan : draft for public review and comment / San Francisco Municipal Railway
documents.creator: San Francisco Municipal Railway
documents.source_label: Internet Archive — San Francisco Public Library partnership scans
documents.gloss: A 2000 Muni short-range transit plan proposes a Cable Car Museum renovation at the Cable Car Barn, 1201 Mason Street, with no schedule or funding yet set.

documents.title: Minutes
documents.creator: San Francisco (Calif.). Municipal Transportation Agency. Board of Directors,San Francisco (Calif.). Parking Authority
documents.source_label: Internet Archive — San Francisco Public Library partnership scans
documents.gloss: 2009 MTA Board minutes authorize an agreement for management and operation of the Cable Car Museum with the Friends of the Cable Car Museum, running to June 30, 2019.

documents.title: Civil Grand Jury reports
documents.creator: California. Grand Jury (San Francisco)
documents.source_label: Internet Archive — San Francisco Public Library partnership scans
documents.gloss: A Civil Grand Jury report examines the 1982 rebuild of the cable car barn and drive system and faults the barn's reconstruction, including a too-low cable-delivery door and unbalanced turntables.

documents.title: Annual report
documents.creator: San Francisco Public Utilities Commission. Hetch Hetchy Water and Power System,San Francisco Public Utilities Commission. Bureau of Light, Heat and Power
documents.source_label: Internet Archive — San Francisco Public Library partnership scans
documents.gloss: A Hetch Hetchy annual report locates the cable-winding machinery and its electric motors in the Cable Car Powerhouse at Washington and Mason Streets.

documents.title: East/West
documents.creator: East/West Pub. Co.
documents.source_label: Internet Archive — San Francisco Public Library partnership scans
documents.gloss: A 1980 East/West notice sets out an $11,787,000 rehabilitation of the Cable Car Barn within a $43,757,000 cable-car program, deemed categorically exempt under CEQA.

documents.title: The data guide, 1990-1991
documents.creator: San Francisco Municipal Railway. Community Affairs Dept
documents.source_label: Internet Archive — San Francisco Public Library partnership scans
documents.gloss: A Muni data guide records the barn at 1201 Mason (83,741 sq ft), built 1887 and rebuilt 1982–84, housing the cable car museum and the 510-hp motors driving the four cables.
